pages.title: Academic Advising | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Academic Advising Center fosters a developmental process of assisting undergraduate students in clarifying and achieving their personal, professional, and educational goals.
contentBlocks: Academic Advising Center offers mandatory freshman advising, general education and graduation requirement advising for all students, and supports mandatory freshman and transfer orientation. The center engages students in a developmental process that helps them clarify and implement individual educational plans consistent with their skills, interests, and values.
New students must attend orientation before making an appointment with the Academic Advising Center.
Please see the " Schedule an Appointment Instructions Sheet " before you schedule an appointment.
Academic Year: M-F 8 am - 6 pm Summer Hours: M-F 8 am - 6 pm
Tues, Apr 28 Lavender Leadership Program (Part 4)
Wed, Apr 29 EMPLOYER ON CAMPUS: Sacramento River Cats and Athletics
Academic Advising Center Lassen Hall, Room 1013 Appointments: Mon. – Fri. 8 a.m. – 6 p.m., Drop–In Services: Mon. – Fri. 8 a.m. – 4 p.m.
Phone: (916) 278-1000 Option #5 advising@csus.edu

links.text: Finish in Four and Through in Two Program

pages.title: Virtual Drop-ins | Sacramento State
pages.description: The page provides the Academic Advising drop-in information for students looking for help with quick advising questions.
contentBlocks: Virtual Drop-ins are designed for quick advising and general education questions for currently enrolled Sac State students only. Appointment slots are held to 10 mins max.
Date: April 27 - May 8 Time: 9 a.m. - 4:45 p.m. Meeting ID: 859 5425 6510
If you have applied to graduate, the AAC is unable to assist you and you must contact your degree advisor .
Academic Year: M-F 8 a.m. - 6 p.m. Summer Hours: M-F 8 a.m. - 6 p.m.

pages.title: General Advising | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Academic Advising Center offers various programs for undeclared, first-year, and second-year.
contentBlocks: Through collaboration and academic guidance, the Academic Advising Center fosters a developmental process of assisting undergraduate students in clarifying and achieving their personal, professional, and educational goals. All freshmen must be advised in both fall and spring semesters.
Advising can take place in 1-to-1 or group sessions.
The link to schedule your appointment will go out via e-mail during the first two weeks of instruction.
The following resources are completed with an Academic Advisor.
Advising Worksheet for Catalog Year Fall 18 or later
Advising worksheet for all other students
Advisors focus on maximizing the courses you need to take by helping you identify the most beneficial general education requirements available for you.
Fall : If you are enrolled in a First Year Seminar, you must complete your first-year advising with your Peer Mentor.
Spring : If you are in at least one of these programs/groups, then you DO NOT need to complete First Year Advising through the Academic Advising Center
College of Engineering & Computer Science (ECS)
Child Development Integrated Pre-Credential Program (Child Development Plan B).
Our goal is to assist students with major exploration in order to ensure timely graduation. Below are steps you can take to declare/explore a major:
Assess your skills, values, and interest
Visit the Career Center for career and major exploration
Declaring a major by 45 units puts you on the right track to graduate in a timely manner. We are here to help you!
Learn About the Declaration of Major Policy

links.text: Advising Worksheet for Catalog Year Fall 18 or later
Advising worksheet for all other students

pages.title: Academic Standing | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides students with Academic Standing Information.
contentBlocks: At the end of each semester, students receive their Academic Standing, which is posted on the unofficial transcript below the grade point average (GPA) calculation. Academic Standing phrases include:
Talking to Your Professors: A Step by Step Guide
Effective Studying and Test Preparation Tips
A student is on Academic Probation when their CSUS GPA or Cumulative GPA is below a 2.0 .
The first semester on academic probation serves as a warning. Any student on probation will be limited to enrolling in no more than 14 units per semester.
Repeat courses that are a D or below. Learn more by reviewing Academic Policies .

links.text: Talking to Your Professors: A Step by Step Guide
Effective Studying and Test Preparation Tips

pages.title: College Success Centers | Sacramento State
pages.description: Major advising is offered by the College Success Centers.
contentBlocks: Major Advising is provided by your college through the College Success Centers:
Social Sciences & Interdisciplinary Studies
Check your Sac State Student Center for your assigned college advisors’ contact information. Your Advisor should be listed above your To Do List.

pages.title: Transfer Resource Program | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Transfer Resource Program provides information and resources for transfer students.
contentBlocks: The Transfer Resource Program provides newly admitted and current transfer students with the opportunity to connect with Professional Advisors and Transfer Peer Coaches (TPCs). Professional Advisors and TPCs assist students in making a smooth academic transition to Sacramento State while helping them engage with the larger campus community. Get connected early and stay on track to graduation. Welcome to Sac State — Go Hornets!
Current Students – Schedule an appointment
Lassen Hall, 1013 Office Hours: Monday–Friday, 8 a.m. – 4 p.m.
Phone: Phone: 916-278-1000, Option #5 transferstudents@csus.edu

pages.title: Second Year Success Program | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information about the Second Year Success Program with the Academic Advising Center at Sacramento State.
headings: Questions or Concerns and Enrolling in ALS 79A
contentBlocks: This program is designed for students in their second year of college who have experienced some academic challenge in their first year. Students are paired with one of our Second Year Success professional staff mentors to provide guidance, support, and advice. We know that each student has the knowledge and ability to be successful and our goal is to ensure that students put their skills and knowledge into action. Through mentorship and student partnership, we aim to ensure college success together.

Second Year Success hosts a 1-unit class every semester to help guide students through the process of exploring majors, developing a plan for their future career, and covering general college life skills such as time management, resume/cover letter practice and financial literacy.

This course is led by a student from the Counseling masters program specializing in Career Counseling. Students participating in the course have seen an increase in their GPA and are more likely to graduate on time.
Please contact the Second Year Success Coordinator
Questions or Concerns and Enrolling in ALS 79A

links.text: Questions or Concerns and Enrolling in ALS 79A

pages.title: Undeclared or Expressed Interest Advising Program | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information about the Undeclared or Expressed Interest Advising Program at Sac State.
headings: Undeclared or Expressed Interest Advising Program
Option 3: Apply to and Be Accepted to an Impacted Program
contentBlocks: The primary goal of the Undeclared/EI Advising Program is to assist 2nd Year/Sophomore students with major and career exploration to ensure a timely declaration of major. We strive to help inform you on program requirements and strategies on how to successfully get into your program of interest or formulate a back-up plan, if necessary. We can also help to connect you to major exploration resources such as those offered by our Career Counselors or to help navigate the process of major declaration once decided.
For any questions or concerns please contact us at undcl-ei-advising@csus.edu
Check your Sac State email for the link to schedule an appointment.
Contact the department of the major you wish to declare for advising and appropriate signatures

If you have completed the impacted major’s prerequisite requirements, submit a supplemental application ASAP. Please visit the department’s website to learn more about their supplemental application period.
Option 3: Apply to and Be Accepted to an Impacted Program

pages.title: Disqualified, Dismissed & TOPSS | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information about the Disqualified/Dismissed & TOPSS advising programs at Sac Stat
headings: Transforming Outcomes Project at Sacramento State (TOPSS)
contentBlocks: When a student is on academic probation, the student will be academically disqualified if their Sacramento State or cumulative GPA after the semester is below a:
Students not on probation will be disqualified if the cumulative GPA is 1.00 or less.
Disqualified students will not be allowed to register unless they are formally reinstated and/or readmitted to the University.
A student reinstated after disqualification who earns a semester GPA below 2.00 or fails to meet other requirements specified in the reinstatement contract will be dismissed.
Academically dismissed students are not eligible for readmission without at least one semester of absence from Sacramento State.
Students who have been dismissed twice must sit out two years before petitioning to return.
Students who have been disqualified or dismissed from the university are eligible to re-apply to return to the Sac State after meeting specific guidelines. Visit the Returning Students page .

A four-year degree completion program administered and delivered inside two California state prisons through a partnership of California State University, Sacramento and the California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation. TOPSS students are earning their BA degree in Communication Studies.
Vanessa Gomez DQ/DM/TOPSS Coordinator v.darling@csus.edu
Disqualification, Dismissal and Reinstatement
Transforming Outcomes Project at Sacramento State (TOPSS)

links.text: Transforming Outcomes Project at Sacramento State (TOPSS)

pages.title: First-Year Programs (FYP) | Sacramento State
pages.description: The page provides information on the First-Year Programs from Academic Advising at Sac State.
headings: First-Year Programs (FYP) Division of Student Affairs
About the Academic Advising: First-Year Programs
contentBlocks: We provide holistic, student-centered advising and mentorship to ensure that students can feel confident in their ability to: navigate campus resources, use online advising tools, explore their major options as they relate to their career goals, and know their degree requirements, so that they can set themselves up for success and for timely graduation.
Need help scheduling advising or figuring out who your assigned advisor is?
E-mail the First-Year Advising coordinators at fya@csus.edu
For students who do not have a Peer Mentor or another mandatory advising program.
This program takes place in-person in Lassen Hall in the Academic Advising Center, and also online via Zoom, both in-person and virtual advising appointments are available Monday through Friday.
General Education/Graduation Requirements to prioritize in the first year:
GE Area 2: Mathematical Concepts and Quantitative Reasoning

pages.title: Finish in Four & Through in Two Advising Team | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information about the California Promise team members and ambassadors.
headings: Finish in Four & Through in Two Advising Team
contentBlocks: The Finish in Four and Through in Two Program enables a specific number of campuses of the California State University (CSU) to establish pledge programs for entering first-time students who are both interested and able to complete baccalaureate degrees in four years.

Many campuses have also established programs for students who are pursuing an Associate Degree for Transfer (ADT) from any California Community College and plan to complete their baccalaureate (bachelor’s) degrees in two years at a CSU campus.
The program is limited to students who meet the criteria:
From communities that are underrepresented in college attendance
Only certain majors at each campus are eligible for this program. Not all students who indicate interest will be able to participate.

Students who commit to enter either the four-year or two-year pledge will be given a priority registration appointment for each state-supported enrollment period and will be provided with routine and thorough academic advisement.

In order to remain in the program, students must meet with their advisors as prescribed, develop an enrollment plan, and complete 30 semester units (or the quarter equivalent) within each academic year, including summer. Participating campuses may stipulate other requirements as well.
Academic Advising Center | GE Catalog options
A student who already applied for graduation

Finish in Four & Through in Two Student Advisors (SSAs) assist students in the CA Promise on their pursuit to timely graduation. By serving as an academic peer resource, SSAs empower students through outreach, guidance, and engagement. SSAs enhance awareness and increase student utilization of the KEYS to Degree toolbox (eAdvising tools), assist students with registration questions, and provide workshops that foster student success.
